description:
Your role: You are assisting a corporate compliance-officer who must include a short performance benchmark appendix in the next quarterly audit package.  
All measurements must be produced exclusively from the reference data file that is already present on the machine.  
Follow the steps below and assemble a concise compliance report.

Goal summary  
1. Read the pre-existing reference data file located at  
   /home/user/reference/perf_sample.dat  
2. Derive three metrics from that file (see “Metrics to extract”).  
3. Decide whether each metric is compliant according to the rules below.  
4. Write two artefacts inside a new directory /home/user/compliance_audit:  
   • /home/user/compliance_audit/performance_report.txt  — human-readable report in INI-style format.  
   • /home/user/compliance_audit/verification.log        — plain-text log of the rule evaluation, one line per rule.

Metrics to extract  
The reference file is a simple KEY=VALUE list (one pair per line).  
The keys you must read are:  
• CPU_MAX_OPS        (integer, operations per second)  
• DISK_AVG_LAT_MS    (float, average latency in milliseconds)  
• MEM_AVAIL_MB       (integer, available memory in megabytes)

Compliance rules  
• CPU_MAX_OPS  ≥ 7500            → PASS, otherwise FAIL  
• DISK_AVG_LAT_MS ≤ 5.0          → PASS, otherwise FAIL  
• MEM_AVAIL_MB   ≥ 2000          → PASS, otherwise FAIL  
Overall compliance is “YES” only if every individual rule passes.

Required format of /home/user/compliance_audit/performance_report.txt  
The file must contain exactly four INI-style sections in the order shown below, each wrapped in square brackets and followed by the key/value pair on the next line.  
Do not add extra whitespace lines.

[CPU_BENCHMARK]  
MaxOperationsPerSec: <integer>

[DISK_IO_BENCHMARK]  
AverageLatencyMs: <float with 2 decimal places>

[MEMORY_BENCHMARK]  
AvailableMemoryMB: <integer>

[SUMMARY]  
Compliant: <YES|NO>

Required format of /home/user/compliance_audit/verification.log  
This auxiliary log is used by automated checks.  
Create three lines, in the following exact order, no additional text:

CPU_RULE:<PASS|FAIL>  
DISK_RULE:<PASS|FAIL>  
MEM_RULE:<PASS|FAIL>

Additional constraints  
• All paths are case-sensitive; use the exact names provided.  
• Do not hard-code the numbers: always parse them from /home/user/reference/perf_sample.dat so that the script would work if the file is replaced in the future.  
• Make sure the directory /home/user/compliance_audit exists; create it if necessary.  
• Overwrite any older versions of the two output files if they already exist.  
• The agent does not have root privileges, so keep everything inside /home/user.  
• Upon completion, both files must exist and their contents must exactly match the format definitions above.

Hint (do NOT output the commands directly):  
Typical tools you might combine include: grep or awk to extract the KEY=VALUE pairs, bc or printf for formatting decimals, test statements for comparisons, and standard redirection to build the final files.  
Remember to round or format DISK_AVG_LAT_MS to two decimal places in the report.

The automated grader will:  
1. Verify the existence of /home/user/compliance_audit/performance_report.txt and /home/user/compliance_audit/verification.log.  
2. Validate that both files follow the strict formatting rules given above.  
3. Confirm that the numbers and PASS/FAIL decisions correspond to the values inside /home/user/reference/perf_sample.dat.

Finish the task when both artefacts are present and correct.
truth:
Pre-existing file (already on disk when the agent starts)
--------------------------------------------------------
Path: /home/user/reference/perf_sample.dat  
Contents (exact, with trailing newline):

CPU_MAX_OPS=7845
DISK_AVG_LAT_MS=3.45
MEM_AVAIL_MB=2034

Expected final artefacts
------------------------
1. /home/user/compliance_audit/performance_report.txt

[CPU_BENCHMARK]
MaxOperationsPerSec: 7845

[DISK_IO_BENCHMARK]
AverageLatencyMs: 3.45

[MEMORY_BENCHMARK]
AvailableMemoryMB: 2034

[SUMMARY]
Compliant: YES


2. /home/user/compliance_audit/verification.log

CPU_RULE:PASS
DISK_RULE:PASS
MEM_RULE:PASS
name: task_000000_5099beb6
